title: Fix audio routing: keep WebAudio frequencies separate from Android res/raw sounds

requirements.text: Update the unified audio routing so that frequency tones (the original/legacy frequencies) are always played via Web Audio oscillator playback and are never delegated to Android `res/raw` playback, even when `window.AndroidAudio` exists.

quotes: bak senin eklediğin sesler ayrı raw dosyasından çalınması gereken sesler ayrı bu ikisini birbirine karıştırma yeninden eski frekanslar doğru düzgün çalsın

acceptanceCriteria: On Android/WebView with `window.AndroidAudio` available, tapping any item in the Frequencies list (e.g., 432 Hz) produces audible output using Web Audio (oscillator-based tone).
Frequencies do not call `window.AndroidAudio.playSound(...)` at all (verify via logs or by code path).

requirements.text: Update the unified audio routing so that non-frequency sounds (kids sleep sounds and peaceful sounds) use Android native playback (`window.AndroidAudio.playSound(soundId)`) when available, and do not attempt Web Audio oscillator playback for these sound IDs.

quotes: senin eklediğin sesler ayrı raw dosyasından çalınması gereken sesler ayrı bu ikisini birbirine karıştırma

acceptanceCriteria: On Android/WebView with `window.AndroidAudio` available, tapping a kids sleep sound or peaceful sound triggers `window.AndroidAudio.playSound(cleanSoundId)` with no file extension.
When playing a non-frequency sound, the app does not run the oscillator-based fallback logic that parses `freq-###`.

requirements.text: Connect the MainPlayer state to a single shared audio manager instance so that play/pause/resume/stop and track changes in `useMainPlayer` consistently start/stop the actual audio output (instead of each component creating its own `useUnifiedAudioManager()` instance).

quotes: yeninden eski frekanslar doğru düzgün çalsın

acceptanceCriteria: When `player.playFrequency(hz)` is called, the audio manager starts playing that frequency; when `player.stop()` is called, audio stops.
MainPlayer pause/resume buttons affect audible playback (pause stops audio; resume restarts the currently selected frequency/sound).
Audio intensity used for visualizations comes from the same single active audio manager instance that is producing sound.

requirements.text: Add clear developer-facing console logs for the routing decision (frequency via WebAudio vs sound via Android native) to make it easy to verify that frequencies and `res/raw` sounds are not being mixed.
acceptanceCriteria: When playing a frequency, logs indicate Web Audio oscillator path and do not indicate Android native path.
When playing a non-frequency sound on Android, logs indicate Android native path and do not indicate oscillator parsing.

constraints: Do not modify files under `frontend/src/components/ui` (compose them as-is).
Do not edit immutable hook files listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT (e.g., `frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts`, `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts`, `frontend/src/main.tsx`).
Backend must remain single-actor; no backend work is required for this audio routing fix.
nonGoals: Do not add new sound content catalogs or new audio files.
Do not implement new third-party audio libraries or external services.
Do not redesign the UI beyond what is necessary to restore correct playback behavior.
